explanation: The requirement states that the system transitions from Start-up Mode to Self Test Mode once (i.e., as soon as) the configurations are loaded and internal checking is terminated. Within the available atomic propositions, 'initDone' signals that configurations have been loaded and checking is terminated, while 'SelfTestMode' designates that the Self Test Mode is active. The correct LTL template to model an instantaneous transition ("once condition, switch state") is template 11: G (initDone -> (X SelfTestMode)), i.e., at every time step, if 'initDone' becomes true, then in the next step, 'SelfTestMode' must become true. This directly and succinctly captures the described transition requirement.

The specified requirement is: When in Start-up Mode and once configurations are loaded and internal checking is terminated ("initDone"), there should be a transition to SelfTestMode. In LTL, this is best captured by stating that whenever we are in StartUpMode and initDone occurs (i.e., both are true), then in the next step, SelfTestMode must be true. Template 41 (G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3))) expresses that globally, whenever two conditions (here: StartUpMode & initDone) are true together, in the next state SelfTestMode must hold. This matches the NL requirement that the transition "shall occur once" the configurations/checking are complete, and ensures no delay between (StartUpMode & initDone) and SelfTestMode. This template cleanly and directly relates the described triggering event to the required state change.

The requirement specifies that the system should transition from Start-up Mode to SelfTest Mode only after the configurations have been loaded and the internal checking is complete. In terms of atomic propositions, 'StartUpMode' should only transition to 'SelfTestMode' after 'initDone' becomes true. The most natural way to express this in LTL is to require that whenever the system is in StartUpMode and 'initDone' becomes true, then (in the next state) the system should transition to SelfTestMode. This is captured by template 11: G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)), with bool_exp2 set as (StartUpMode & initDone) (i.e., whenever both StartUpMode is true and initDone is true), and bool_exp3 as SelfTestMode (i.e., in the next state, SelfTestMode is true). N_DURATION is not needed for this template.

The requirement states that the transition from Start-up Mode to SelfTest Mode should occur once configurations have been loaded and internal checking is terminated—that is, when 'initDone' becomes true. We want that whenever the system is in StartUpMode, as soon as 'initDone' is true, SelfTestMode should hold in the next step. This is best modeled by ensuring that always, if 'StartUpMode' is true and 'initDone' is true, then in the next state 'SelfTestMode' must be true. This directly expresses the natural language description in terms of the provided atomic propositions.

The property describes the required transition from Start-up Mode to SelfTest Mode. According to the specification, this transition should only occur after the configurations have been loaded and the internal checking has finished (i.e., when 'initDone' is true). Using the atomic propositions, we interpret this as: whenever the system is in StartUpMode and 'initDone' becomes true, then in the next state, SelfTestMode must be true. Template 11 captures the conditional transition: globally, whenever (StartUpMode & initDone) holds, SelfTestMode must be true in the next state.

The requirement specifies that the system must transition from Start-up Mode to SelfTest Mode (i.e., enter SelfTestMode) once 'the configurations have been loaded and the internal checking is terminated' (i.e., when initDone becomes true). This is best captured by stating: Whenever initDone holds, the SelfTestMode condition should be true in the next time step. Thus, the corresponding LTL property states that globally, if initDone holds, then in the next step SelfTestMode holds.

The input requirement states that the system should transition from Start-up Mode to Self Test Mode once both (a) configurations have been loaded and (b) internal checking has been terminated. The atomic proposition 'initDone' closely matches this event ("indicates the configurations have been loaded and the internal checking is terminated"). The desired behavior is that once 'initDone' becomes true, the system should enter Self Test Mode, which is captured by SelfTestMode. Thus, the correct LTL formula is: globally, if 'initDone' is true, then in the next state, SelfTestMode is true. This ensures the transition is triggered as required by the input specification.

The provided natural language requirement describes a transition from Start-up Mode to SelfTest Mode, which should occur once both (1) the configurations have been loaded and internal checking is finished (represented by 'initDone'), and (2) the system is currently in StartUpMode. The next state should be SelfTestMode. This is a typical transition requirement where, under a specific trigger condition ('initDone' and 'StartUpMode'), the system must move to another mode/state ('SelfTestMode').

To capture this logic, I chose template 11: 'G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3))' -- which means globally, whenever the triggering condition (here, bool_exp2) holds, in the next step, bool_exp3 must hold. The mapping is: bool_exp2 = 'StartUpMode & initDone' (we are in StartUpMode and configurations are loaded/internal checking done), bool_exp3 = 'SelfTestMode' (SelfTest Mode is reached). N_DURATION is not needed for this template, so it's null.

This template captures the one-step transition guarantee between StartUpMode and SelfTestMode upon the completion of loading and internal checks, following the requirement as stated.

The requirement states that the transition from Start-up Mode to SelfTest Mode should occur once the configurations have been loaded and the internal checking is terminated. In terms of the atomic propositions, 'StartUpMode' represents the controller being in Start-up Mode, 'SelfTestMode' represents the controller being in SelfTest Mode, and 'initDone' encodes that configurations have been loaded and internal checking is finished. The template G (initDone -> SelfTestMode) captures that globally, whenever the initialization is complete (initDone), the system moves to (is in) SelfTest Mode. This directly expresses the required functional transition based on the atomic propositions available.

The requirement states that the mode must transition from Start-up Mode to SelfTest Mode once certain conditions (configurations loaded and internal checking terminated) are met. The atomic proposition 'initDone' matches 'configurations have been loaded and the internal checking is terminated', and 'SelfTestMode' matches 'Self test Mode'. Hence, the LTL property should specify that whenever 'initDone' becomes true, eventually 'SelfTestMode' becomes true (i.e., the transition to SelfTestMode happens). This is best expressed with a response pattern: globally, if 'initDone' is true, then eventually 'SelfTestMode' becomes true.
